macro_segments.title: Introduction to the Mishna on Tam and Muad: Payment Levels and the Meaning of Aliya

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks on Daf Yud-Zayin and the structure of the perek
Mishna: tam pays half damages from the animal's body, muad pays full damages from aliya
Gemara: What does aliya mean? Rabbi Elazar interprets it as the best of one's property
Scriptural support: Chizkiyahu's burial in the elevated graves of the Davidic line

macro_segments.title: Aggadic Digression on Sefer Torah, Learning, Teaching, and Acts of Righteousness

micro_segments.title: Transition to aggadic material about Chizkiyahu through interpretation of the word zanim (spices)
Rabbi Eliezer and Rabbi Shmuel bar Nachmani on the types of fragrances in Asa's grave
Wordplay connecting zanim (spices) to zona (harlot) and the pit metaphor in Yirmiyahu
Rava's homily on stumbling blocks and tzedaka: not vetting recipients
The special honor done to Chizkiyahu: placing a Sefer Torah on his bier and declaring he fulfilled it
Dispute between R. Natan and other Tannaim on duration of learning by graves: three, seven, or thirty days
Chizkiyahu's funeral procession: 36,000 people with uncovered shoulders as code from the word asu lo
Story of Rabbah bar bar Chana following Rabbi Yochanan and the latter's procedure after using the bathroom
Debate over hierarchy of religious value: learning versus doing; Rashi vs. Tosafot on which is greater
Resolution: teaching is higher than learning or doing; modeling the hierarchy in one's actions
Midrash on Yeshayahu 32:20 on one who engages in Torah and gmilut chasadim; connection to inheritance of two tribes

macro_segments.title: Introduction to the Second Perek: Regel (Foot Damage) and the Definition of Muad

micro_segments.title: Transition to Perek Two and structure of Bava Kamma: moving from general principles to specific cases
Mishna: How is regel muad? When an animal breaks things in the normal course of walking
Mishna: Examples of regel muad—kicking, tzrorot, trampling vessels, and damage to other vessels struck by those vessels
Mishna: Behema muad to walk and break; tarnegolim muad to walk and break; exceptions involving buckets and scattered items

macro_segments.title: Gemara Discussion of Regel and Behema Redundancy; Introduction of Av and Toladah

micro_segments.title: Ravina's challenge to Rava: why repeat regel and behema with identical language?
Rava's response: the Tanna teaches an av (the leg itself) and a toladah (any part of the animal's body)
Gemara raises further questions: why not structure the mishna differently if av and toladah are intended?
Rav Ashi's resolution: the first mishna concerns chaya (untamed animals) and the second concerns behema (tame animals)
Gemara resolution of av/toladah ordering and structure through literary considerations

macro_segments.title: Braita Enumerating Cases of Direct Body Damage (Gufo) versus Indirect Damage (Tzrorot)

micro_segments.title: Braita: behema muad to break in walking; clarification that damage via body, tail, hair, saddle, bit, and collar bell are all gufo
Braita: case of donkey bearing a burden—pays full damages as gufo, not tzrorot
Sumchus disputes on tzrorot cases: pebbles scattered and pig rooting in dung heap
Gemara resolves Sumchus: tzrorot of regel pays half by default; Sumchus holds it pays full

macro_segments.title: Cases of Regel Damage via Animals in Motion: Wings, Jumping, Pecking, and Wind

micro_segments.title: Braita: chickens flying break vessels with wings (full damage); wind from wings breaks vessels (half damage)
Braita: chickens jumping on dough and fruit—full damage for pecking/jumping; half for resulting dust and pebbles
Braita: chicken flying with wind from wings breaking vessels—half damage (consistent with wind principle)

macro_segments.title: Rava's Explanation of Kocho vs. Gufo Using the Analogy of Zav and Tumah

micro_segments.title: Rava's principle: if by a zav the case would be tamei, it is gufo (full damage); if tahor, it is kocho (half damage)
Application: a calf drawing a wagon over vessels—gufo because a zav on the wagon would make vessels tamei (through midras)
Application: pebbles scattered from under a wagon—tzrorot (kocho) because a zav would not make the vessel tamei via scattered pebbles
Braita aligns with Rava: calf drawing wagon pays full; similar structure with other examples
Case of chickens pecking a bucket rope: full damage because the rope is connected to the bucket

macro_segments.title: Conclusion: Rava's Unresolved Question on Similar Cases

micro_segments.title: Rava raises a ba'ei (question) about a similar case to be explored in the next session

topical_tags.term: aliya (best property, full damages from owner's assets)
Chizkiyahu (king of Yehuda, honored in death)
gufo (body/direct damage by the animal itself)
halacha l'Moshe mi-Sinai (law given to Moses at Sinai)
zav (one with a genital discharge, rules of tumah)
Rabbi Eliezer (Tanna disputing on types of spices)
Rabbi Shmuel bar Nachmani (Amora on erotic fragrances and adultery interpretation)
Rabbi Natan (disputing duration of learning by grave)
Rabbah bar bar Chana (Amora relating story of R. Yochanan)
Rabbi Yochanan (Amora on hierarchy of learning/teaching)
Rashi (commentator, on gufo vs. kocho and hierarchy of values)
Tosafot (medieval commentators, on hierarchy of learning)
Rava (Amora, major authority on nezikin principles)
Sumchus (Tanna with minority view on tzrorot damages)
bor (pit, one of the four avot nezikin)
arba'ah avot nezikin (four categories of damaging agents)
